text: As I stand outside the glass enclosure, looking into the R+D space, I feel a sense of curiosity mixed with a quiet apprehension. The bright, sterile lights reflect the tension of observation—am I an observer, or am I also being observed? My own presence here becomes part of the scene: one who witnesses, yet might be witnessed by others behind the glass. There is a certain isolation to this feeling, a liminal space between knowing and not knowing, between peering in and being kept out.
